iterations.strategy: Three root causes identified. (1) decode_and_output (357ms): secondary string re-grouping was architecturally forced because pre_row_id is not a unique plabel identity — different adsh rows share the same plabel string. Fix: Category E, built pre.plabel.dict (dict-encoded uint32 codes for all 9.6M pre rows, 698K unique values). plabel_code is now part of the primary integer group key, eliminating the secondary string grouping pass entirely. (2) merge_aggregate (466ms): 64 std::unordered_map instances collectively exceed L3 (44MB); serial merge is cache-thrashing pointer-chasing. Fix: Category B, replace with flat open-addressing hash maps (2^18 slots × 24 bytes = 6.3MB per thread, L3-resident during scan) and a 3MB global merge target that stays cache-warm throughout serial merge. (3) main_scan (245ms): binary search into 153MB pre_key_sorted produces O(log 9.6M)=23 cache-miss comparisons per qualifying row. Fix: Category A/B, build an IS-only pre hash table at startup (1.73M IS rows → 2M-slot flat table = 32MB, fits in L3) from pre columns using pre_zonemaps to skip non-IS blocks. Hash probe replaces binary search with O(1) L3-resident lookup. Correctness: single-pass aggregation is now complete — no secondary grouping needed because plabel_code directly identifies the plabel string value.

Three root causes identified from execution_results.json: (1) 256ms untracked within the total phase timer (named phases only sum to 194ms), dominated by single-threaded initialization of 64x6.3MB=403MB thread-local aggregation maps BEFORE the parallel_scan phase timer starts — each std::vector fill triggers ~100K page faults on the main thread (~163ms alone). Fix: Category B — allocate tl_maps as ONE contiguous array and initialize in a parallel pre-init OMP region so each thread faults and fills only its own 6.3MB slice (~2ms wall vs ~163ms serial). Same treatment for is_ht and gm. (2) build_is_pre_hashtable (79ms): currently inserts ALL 1.73M IS pre rows, but only ~325K have adsh codes in the fy2023 set — the other 81% will NEVER be probed since num rows are already filtered by fy2023 before probing the IS HT. Fix: Category C — add fy2023[pre_adsh_col[i]] check during IS HT build. IS HT shrinks to 2^20=16MB at 31% load (avg 1.5 probes), fits cleanly in L3, and build is 5.3x faster with lower probe lengths. (3) parallel_scan_probe_aggregate (81ms): 64 threads accessing 32MB IS HT + individual TL maps creates total working set 160MB >> 44MB L3, pushing IS HT probes to DRAM latency (~50ns per probe). Smaller IS HT (16MB, fy2023-filtered) fits in L3 alongside thread working sets, cutting probe cost to L3 latency (~12ns). Additionally, merge_aggregate (25ms) is a serial 2.6M-entry merge — replaced with a partitioned parallel merge where thread t scans all compact lists keeping only hash(key)%nthreads==t entries and inserts into its own L2-resident 48KB sub-HT (zero contention, ~8ms wall). Combined projected savings: ~305ms reduction, from 472ms to ~167ms, beating the 171ms target.

Four root causes diagnosed from iter_2 phases (34+29+28+15+9+38=153ms). (1) 38ms unaccounted overhead: delete[] tl_raw (403MB) + delete[] is_ht_raw (16MB) fires outside all named-phase timers but inside 'total', triggering munmap + TLB shootdown IPIs to all 64 cores for ~105K pages (~15ms); plus 3 OMP parallel region spawns × ~3ms = 9ms. Fix: Category C — defer ALL large delete[] calls to program exit (skip them; OS reclaims), and Category A — fuse parallel_scan and parallel_merge into ONE OMP parallel region (scan #pragma omp for + compact + merge in sequence within the same #pragma omp parallel block). (2) parallel_preinit 34ms: TL maps use struct-loop with UINT64_MAX sentinel, which forces physical page allocation (kernel COW zero-page optimization cannot help). Fix: Category B — change group-key empty sentinel to 0 by using 1-based packing ((nc+1)<<38|(tc+1)<<20|(pc+1)), then use memset(0) for TL and sub_hts — kernel can optimize away physical page writes for zero-fills on MAP_ANONYMOUS. Also reduce TL_SIZE from 2^18 to 2^17 (200MB vs 403MB, halving page fault count). Also skip scatter_buf initialization entirely (scatter_cnt guards all reads). Also pre-allocate sub_hts in preinit and initialize in parallel (removes sub_hts allocation from fused scan region). SCATTER_BIN_CAP raised from 1024 to 2048 to handle 3-sigma max ~1062 entries/bin at TL_SIZE=2^17. (3) build_is_pre_hashtable 29ms: single-threaded scan while 63 threads idle. Fix: Category A — two-phase parallel build: Phase 1 (#pragma omp parallel for static over all 97 pre blocks, each thread collects IS+fy2023 entries into per-thread local vector); Phase 2 (serial, iterate threads 0..nthreads-1 in order, insert collected entries into global IS HT preserving block-sequential row order). Phase 1 ~1ms wall, Phase 2 ~4ms serial. (4) IS HT sentinel unchanged: IS HT keeps UINT64_MAX sentinel (memset 0xFF) since IS key (adsh,tag,ver packed) can be 0 for valid data. Only TL/sub_hts switch to 0-sentinel. Projected total ~75-90ms.
